_meta.description: Forensic rules base for probabilistic AI video analysis. Never returns binary verdict — always ai_probability (0-100) + confidence (low/medium/high) + nuanced verdict.
_meta.philosophy: This system helps a human understand if a video COULD be AI. It does not claim 100% certainty. Natural compression artifacts can generate false positives. Always cross-reference with other sources.

output_format.confidence: low | medium | high — based on layer agreement
evidence: list of strings describing detected anomalies with localization

confidence_thresholds.note: If confidence=low, verdict is ALWAYS uncertain, regardless of probability. Confidence is also lowered if spread between layers > 35 points.
confidence_thresholds.spread_rule: If max_score - min_score > 35 among active layers → confidence = low automatically

verdict_thresholds.note: These thresholds only apply if confidence != low. With low confidence, verdict is always uncertain.

layers.description: Inter-frame noise coherence (Laplacian), chroma uniformity, DCT 8×8 block artifacts. Resistant to H.264 compression as it compares relative profiles rather than absolute values.
8 consecutive frames over 2-3s in median third. Measures local inter-frame difference (mean + CV). Sora/Runway over-interpolation = mean<1.5 AND cv<0.08.
Ignored if music detected or silence >70%. 5 signals: uniform background noise, hard cuts, ZCR frequencies (speech), dynamic energy, TTS pause pattern.
Strongest layer. LLaVA describes 3 forensic frames, LLaMA produces structured verdict with individual flags (anatomy, text, texture, lighting, motion).

combo_rules._description: Applied AFTER weighted average. Each rule adjusts raw score and is listed in result's combos array.

three_layers_ai.confidence_effect: reinforces medium → high if strong agreement

anatomy_in_evidence.rationale: Anatomical errors are currently the most reliable AI signal

morphing_in_evidence.rationale: Inter-frame morphing = AI video signature (absent in real videos)

organic_in_evidence.rationale: Organic variation = positive signal for real video

text_illegibility.evidence_template: frame X: illegible or incoherent text

motion_artifacts.evidence_template: frame X: morphing or inter-frame artifacts

natural_indicators.evidence_template: frame X: natural indicators of real capture

noise_coherence.description: CV of Laplacian residual across frames. < 0.05 = highly suspicious, > 0.25 = natural

over_smooth_local.condition: mean < 1.5 AND cv < 0.08 over local window
over_smooth_local.evidence_template: temporal — AI over-interpolation on local window (Sora/Runway signature)

suspicious_smooth.evidence_template: temporal — abnormal smoothness for active scene

natural_motion.evidence_template: temporal — natural motion with organic micro-variation

static_inconclusive.note: Static scene = inconclusive. Do not penalize.

music_bypass.description: If bass > 180 ZCR/s AND treble > 1800 ZCR/s AND sustained RMS → music → layer ignored
music_bypass.note: Videos with background music gave false 'real' positives. This bypass avoids corrupting the score.

silence_bypass.description: If > 70% of samples are below silence threshold → layer ignored

tts_pause_pattern.description: CV of inter-word silences. < 0.15 = very regular TTS. > 0.55 = natural speech.

noise_floor_uniformity.description: CV of RMS over 100ms windows. < 0.05 = uniform (TTS). > 0.40 = natural.

known_limitations.deepfake_faceswap: Face-swaps on real video can score low. The underlying video is real, only the face is synthetic. LLaVA is the best layer to detect these.
known_limitations.top_tier_ai: Best generations from Sora v2/Kling Ultra can fall into ambiguous range (40-60%). The field evolves quickly.
known_limitations.compressed_real: Heavily recompressed real video can show pixel artifacts. Inter-frame approach (vs absolute values) minimizes this risk.
known_limitations.music_tracks: Videos with background music: audio layer ignored. Score calculated on 3 layers max.
known_limitations.static_scenes: Static scene = temporal inconclusive. Normal, not a bug.
known_limitations.short_videos: Videos < 3s may lack frames for dense temporal analysis.
